name: Goddard House Assisted Living & Memory Care
notes: Pioneer in EnrichedLIFE philosophy. On-site Gallery M. Competency-certified for LGBT elders.
pitch_hook: This 45-minute workshop uses the forward-and-backward walking rhythms of Argentine Tango to target motor-cognitive pathways associated with gait stability and working memory. Your residents will experience a boost in core strength and a reduction in fall-related anxiety, all while fostering the deep social connections central to the EnrichedLIFE mission.

name: Center Communities of Brookline
notes: Hebrew SeniorLife network. Follow up regarding schedule availability.
pitch_hook: Leverage the research of the Hinda and Arthur Marcus Institute for Aging Research by introducing a Tango workshop shown to outperform traditional exercise for Parkinson's and dementia patients. This session promotes neuroplasticity and bone density through weight-bearing movement, offering a high-culture experience aligned with Brookline's intellectual spirit.

name: Susan Bailis Assisted Living
notes: Urban community near Symphony Hall. Activities team personally invites residents to live performances. Performance date TBD.
pitch_hook: Capitalize on your residents' proximity to the arts with an Argentine Tango performance that doubles as a therapeutic intervention for balance and coordination. This workshop provides a restaurant-style cultural experience, releasing feel-good endorphins and strengthening the social bonds that help your urban community thrive.

name: Cadbury Commons
notes: Family-owned. Active independence model. Strong intellectual and arts focus. Cambridge lifelong learner population.
pitch_hook: This workshop transforms the aesthetic beauty of Tango into a clinical tool for cognitive flexibility. By challenging Cambridge's lifelong learners to coordinate complex patterns with a partner, the session stimulates neural pathways and improves spatiotemporal awareness, directly addressing sensory decline in aging.

name: Neville Place
notes: Located on Fresh Pond Reservation. Research-based Compass Memory Support program. Strong fit for therapeutic Tango.
pitch_hook: Specifically designed for memory care, this Tango workshop uses rhythmic synchronization to bypass verbal barriers and reach residents through music and touch. This non-pharmacological approach creates an enriched environment that activates long-term memories and improves quality of life for those with cognitive impairment.

name: Standish Village
notes: Award-winning. Known for nurturing connections and innovative seven-day-a-week programming.
pitch_hook: Introduce your residents to the close embrace of Tango to foster empathy and alleviate the feelings of isolation associated with memory loss. This session targets functional mobility and gait, providing a cardiovascular workout that supports your mission of high-quality, person-centered care.

name: Atria Marina Place
notes: Features a Platinum Series for seniors with professional-level entertainment and cultural outings.
pitch_hook: Elevate your Platinum Series with a workshop that combines the social grace of a cocktail hour with the clinical benefits of a physical therapy session. The Tango provides sustained attention and postural alignment, ensuring your active residents maintain the stability needed for their coastal lifestyle.

name: Compass on the Bay
notes: Focused exclusively on memory care. Implements Compass Programming for therapeutic activities.
pitch_hook: Use the rhythmic walking of the Tango to engage the working memory of your residents. This attentional task has been shown to reduce agitation and improve mood in dementia care, creating a psychological state of well-being that lasts well after the music stops.

name: Benchmark Senior Living at Waltham Crossings
notes: Casual atmosphere. Wide variety of social activities. Community-building focus.
pitch_hook: This 45-minute Tango session provides a high-energy alternative to card games, offering a weight-bearing activity that maintains bone density and improves endurance. It is a powerful community-building tool that encourages residents to stay connected through physical expression and shared rhythm.

name: Springhouse Senior Living
notes: Resident-led cultural committees. Strong partnerships with Boston's artistic community.
pitch_hook: This performance and workshop will appeal to your residents' intellectual curiosity by blending the history of the Tango with a hands-on lesson in mind-body coordination. It supports your holistic approach to wellness by improving balance and reducing stress through immersive musical engagement.

name: Hebrew Rehabilitation Center at NewBridge
notes: Intergenerational campus. First-class educational and cultural programming. Hebrew SeniorLife network.
pitch_hook: Perfect for your intergenerational setting, the Tango is a social bridge that connects residents through the universal language of dance. This session addresses the motor-cognitive disconnect common in neurological conditions, providing a professional-grade cultural event that supports the health of the entire campus.

name: Orchard Cove
notes: Resident-driven community. Independent and enhanced living. Pondside campus.
pitch_hook: In alignment with your resident-driven culture, this workshop empowers residents to master a complex new skill that enhances physical and mental agility. It provides a cardiovascular workout that improves posture and coordination, helping your residents live their best lives on their beautiful campus.

name: Sophia Snow House
notes: Vibrant, service-oriented community. Strong staff culture prioritizing social vitality.
pitch_hook: This workshop is an immersive experience that releases endorphins and promotes relaxation, serving as a powerful stress reliever. It fosters a profound sense of connection among partners, enriching the social fabric through creative and physical expression.

name: Chestnut Park at Cleveland Circle
notes: High-end urban lifestyle. Brighton/Brookline border. Access to Boston cultural amenities.
pitch_hook: This Argentine Tango session brings the professional stage to your community, offering a sophisticated performance and cardiovascular workout that improves endurance and stamina. It is an effective intervention for cognitive health, challenging residents to memorize patterns and synchronize movements.

name: Ruggles Assisted Living
notes: Community-based. Heavy emphasis on active engagement and dignity.
pitch_hook: This Tango performance is a therapeutic care tool that combats the inactivity and isolation often faced by urban seniors. It encourages residents to stimulate their potential physically and socially, providing a measurable boost to quality of life through the magic of rhythm and touch.

name: The Residences at Wingate
notes: Continuum of care including Mind & Memory Care neighborhood.
pitch_hook: Specifically tailored for your Mind & Memory Care neighborhood, this workshop uses Tango as a non-pharmacological therapy to improve balance and walking ability. It activates neural pathways through music and rhythmic walking, providing residents with an aesthetic sense of pleasure and well-being.

name: Hale House
notes: Warm, family-like environment. Personalized care with professional-level social supports.
pitch_hook: This intimate workshop fosters the connection and expression central to Hale House's philosophy. By focusing on the close embrace and core stability, we provide a session that reduces back pain and improves posture, allowing residents to enjoy a fuller, healthier life.

name: 2Life Communities
notes: Inclusive and vibrant. Emphasizes joyful experiences and connected community. Active social day programs.
pitch_hook: This socio-cultural experience brings residents together to share an aesthetic space and build community. The Tango serves as a holistic approach to preventing functional decline, offering a fun and accessible way for residents to improve their balance and mood.

name: Maplewood at Weston
notes: High-tech, high-service facility. Uses advanced cognitive health tracking (REACT Neuro) to personalize care.
pitch_hook: Complement your residents' cognitive health profiles with a workshop shown to boost mental agility and cognitive flexibility. The Tango demands precise coordination and mindful focus, serving as a physical counterpart to your personalized care plans.

name: Kisco Senior Living at The Falls
notes: Dedicated memory care unit. Activities Director leads innovative therapeutic programming.
pitch_hook: This workshop is a therapeutic activity program designed to support emotional and social well-being through rhythmic walking and imitation. It addresses the motor-cognitive disconnect in dementia care, creating a vibrant, engaging atmosphere that meets both clinical and social needs.

name: The Cambridge Homes
notes: Nonprofit with active cultural programs. Community-centered approach.
pitch_hook: This Tango workshop aligns perfectly with your cultural enrichment mission, offering residents a sophisticated program that improves balance and fosters social connection. The rhythmic structure of Tango engages motor-cognitive pathways in ways that standard programming cannot.

name: Scandinavian Living Center
notes: Community-centered. Actively encourages outside groups to visit and perform.
pitch_hook: This workshop brings an international cultural art form to your community, celebrating the spirit of connection that defines your center. Tango's emphasis on partnership and rhythm creates a joyful, therapeutic experience that improves balance and lifts the mood of every resident.

name: Waterstone at Circle
notes: High-end community. Regularly hosts concerts and lectures for residents.
pitch_hook: Elevate your concert and lecture series with an Argentine Tango performance that doubles as a therapeutic intervention. This sophisticated program provides the cultural richness your residents expect while delivering measurable improvements in balance, cognitive health, and social well-being.

name: Cambridge Center for Adult Education (CCAE)
notes: Landmark institution in Harvard Square. Diverse classes in arts and humanities. Lifelong learner community.
pitch_hook: Our Tango workshop is a mindful exploration of movement that supports neuroplasticity and cognitive health in your community of lifelong learners. This session provides a sophisticated socio-cultural experience aligned with your mission of inviting everyone to learn something new.

name: JCC Greater Boston
notes: Mission-driven. Rich tapestry of cultural programming. Wellness Speaker Series. Inclusive community.
pitch_hook: This 45-minute workshop is a perfect addition to your Wellness Speaker Series, offering a scientifically-backed method for improving balance and strength. It combines the joy of dance with a cardiovascular workout, helping members stay active while building the deep social connections central to the JCC mission.

name: Cambridge Arts Council
notes: Municipal arts department. Summer in the City series. Focus on civic engagement and public art.
pitch_hook: As part of your Summer in the City series, this workshop uses Tango as a tool for civic engagement and community building. It offers a multisensorial intervention that promotes health and wellness for artists and residents alike, demonstrating how movement can foster equity and accessibility in public spaces.

name: Quincy Parks & Recreation
notes: Wide range of adult educational and recreational programs. Health-focused community mission.
pitch_hook: Elevate your adult programs with a Tango workshop that targets core strength and proprioception, essential for long-term mobility and fall prevention. This session offers a unique cultural performance that fits your mission of providing diverse and engaging social opportunities for all residents.

name: Brookline Adult and Community Education (BACE)
notes: One of the oldest public education programs in MA. Dance, ceramics, painting. Community-focused.
pitch_hook: This Tango workshop provides an immersive and rhythmic experience that allows students to master a complex new skill while improving physical fitness and mental agility. It is a powerful stress reliever that promotes emotional well-being, aligning with your long history of self-sustaining, community-focused education.

name: South Shore Art Center (SSAC)
notes: Nonprofit dedicated to art education. Creative renewal for adults. Located near Quincy border.
pitch_hook: This performance and workshop explores the posture and frame of the human body as a living work of art. It offers a unique form of creative renewal that challenges participants to connect through rhythm and touch, enriching their artistic practice through the study of movement and synchronization.

name: New Art Center
notes: Over 60 annual art programs. Serving students from Boston, Brookline, and beyond.
pitch_hook: Integrate the art of movement into your curriculum with a Tango workshop shown to ignite creativity and boost cognitive health. This session offers a holistic approach to wellness, teaching participants to coordinate movements with a partner in an environment that values making, viewing, and experiencing art.

name: Community Art Center
notes: Professional development and community-based art training. Focus on historically underserved populations.
pitch_hook: This Tango workshop serves as a culturally and linguistically accessible intervention that promotes community empowerment through the arts. By offering a shared aesthetic space and fostering social bonding, we provide a transformative experience that supports the resilience and well-being of the Cambridge community.

name: Quincy Art Association
notes: Adult and children's classes. Watercolor, oil painting, portrait drawing. Community arts focus.
pitch_hook: This workshop presents Argentine Tango as a moving portrait, focusing on the fundamentals of form and connection. It offers your members a social and cultural event that stimulates the senses and improves mind-body coordination, providing a dynamic alternative to traditional studio classes.

name: Boston Center for the Arts (BCA)
notes: Contemporary arts hub. Supports artists through innovative programming and residency programs.
pitch_hook: This Tango performance and workshop showcases the transformative power of the arts. It provides a vital cultural program that engages a broad audience, promoting well-being and social connection through professional therapeutic dance—a natural fit for BCA's mission to support artists and their communities.
